 {"id":"bd-1w6k.8","title":"CI + Docs: Keep RealDB E2E Suite Healthy","description":"Make the suite runnable by future us:

 {"id":"bd-1wrv","title":"Benchmark: results collector and Markdown comparison report","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:21.084870723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:05:38.853033466Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:05:38.853012606Z","close_reason":"Implemented bench results collector + Markdown/JSON report as crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/bench_report.rs (reads Criterion target/criterion estimates/benchmark JSON, pairs csqlite vs fsqlite, computes speedup table; --json for dashboard consumption). Wired bin in crates/fsqlite-e2e/Cargo.toml (bench-report) and ensured gates (fmt/check/clippy/test) green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wrv","depends_on_id":"bd-1dus","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:31.451627454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wrv","depends_on_id":"bd-1fez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:32.167673260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wrv","depends_on_id":"bd-3rze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:31.752991744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wrv","depends_on_id":"bd-6d9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:32.378633848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1wrv","depends_on_id":"bd-72im","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:31.958057220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1049,"issue_id":"bd-1wrv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task title: Benchmark: results collector and Markdown comparison report 
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing e2e harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:09Z"},{"id":1068,"issue_id":"bd-1wrv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Task: Benchmark — results collector and Markdown comparison report

### What
Build a post-benchmark utility that aggregates all Criterion results and generates a human-readable Markdown comparison report.

### Report Format
```markdown
# FrankenSQLite vs C SQLite Benchmark Report
Generated: 2026-02-09 | Machine: ... | Rust: nightly-...

## Summary Table

| Benchmark | FrankenSQLite | C SQLite | Speedup |
|-----------|--------------|----------|---------|
| Sequential INSERT (10K rows) | 45,000 rows/s | 42,000 rows/s | 1.07x |
| Concurrent INSERT (8 threads) | 180,000 rows/s | 48,000 rows/s | 3.75x |
| Mixed OLTP (read throughput) | 120,000 qps | 115,000 qps | 1.04x |
| Large transaction (1M rows) | 12.3s | 11.8s | 0.96x |

## Detailed Results

### Sequential Write Throughput
[Criterion violin plot here]
...
```

### Data Sources
- Criterion JSON output in `target/criterion/`
- Custom metrics logged during benchmarks (SQLITE_BUSY counts, memory, etc.)

### Implementation
- Rust binary in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/bench_report.rs`
- Reads Criterion `estimates.json` files from `target/criterion/` directories
- Computes speedup ratios
- Generates Markdown table
- Also outputs JSON for TUI dashboard

### Acceptance Criteria
- Report generates automatically after benchmark suite
- Includes all benchmark categories
- Speedup ratios calculated correctly
- Markdown renders well on GitHub
- JSON output for TUI consumption
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:44Z"}]}
